mnemonics.title: Cube Components
mnemonics.content: CUBE: Corners (8), Unchanged (6 faces), Borders (12 edges), Each face square

mnemonics.title: Transformation Types
mnemonics.content: MRS T: Model → Rotate → Scale → Translate (order of operations)

mnemonics.title: Rendering Pipeline Steps
mnemonics.content: VICER: Vertex processing → Primitive Assembly → Clipping → Rasterization → Fragment processing

acronyms.usage: Coordinate system from -1 to 1 used before viewport transformation
Stores depth values for hidden surface removal
Three main transformations in rendering pipeline
Number of independent parameters for object transformation (6 for rigid body)
Angular extent of the scene visible to camera

visualTips.visualization: Think of a Rubik's cube - each corner has x, y, z coordinates like (+1,+1,+1), (-1,-1,-1)
Imagine facing a door - you see the front, the back is culled. CCW winding faces camera, CW is back-facing.
The 4x4 matrix is like a coordinate transformer machine: input vertex → matrix multiplication → transformed vertex output
Think of two chalkboards - write on back one while showing front, then flip to show your work
Stack of transparent sheets - each object draws on its corresponding depth layer, closer sheets cover farther ones

analogies.analogy: Like corners (vertices) and panels (faces) of a soccer ball - vertices are points, faces are the polygons between them
Like a person in their room (model space) vs their address in a city (world space) - same person, different reference point
Like a factory assembly line - object enters, goes through transformation stations, emerges as final image
Like looking through a stack of photographs - the closest one (smallest Z) is what you see
Flat is like a diamond (distinct facets), smooth is like a marble sphere (continuous surface)
